authors_text: Jingjie Li,Sunpreet Singh Arora,Kassem Fawaz,Younghyun Kim,Can Liu,Sebastian Meiser,Mohsen Minaei,Maliheh Shirvanian,Kim Wagner
title: How Interactions Influence Users' Security Perception of Virtual Reality Authentication?
title_zh: 交互如何影响用户对虚拟现实认证的安全感知？
abstract: Users readily embrace the rapid advancements in virtual reality (VR) technology within various everyday contexts, such as gaming, social interactions, shopping, and commerce. In order to facilitate transactions and payments, VR systems require access to sensitive user data and assets, which consequently necessitates user authentication. However, there exists a limited understanding regarding how users' unique experiences in VR contribute to their perception of security. In our study, we adopt a research approach known as ``technology probe'' to investigate this question. Specifically, we have designed probes that explore the authentication process in VR, aiming to elicit responses from participants from multiple perspectives. These probes were seamlessly integrated into the routine payment system of a VR game, thereby establishing an organic study environment. Through qualitative analysis, we uncover the interplay between participants' interaction experiences and their security perception. Remarkably, despite encountering unique challenges in usability during VR interactions, our participants found the intuitive virtualized authentication process beneficial and thoroughly enjoyed the immersive nature of VR. Furthermore, we observe how these interaction experiences influence participants' ability to transfer their pre-existing understanding of authentication into VR, resulting in a discrepancy in perceived security. Moreover, we identify users' conflicting expectations, encompassing their desire for an enjoyable VR experience alongside the assurance of secure VR authentication. Building upon our findings, we propose recommendations aimed at addressing these expectations and alleviating potential conflicts.

Specifically, we have designed probes that explore the authentication process in VR, aiming to elicit responses from participants from multiple perspectives. These probes were seamlessly integrated into the routine payment system of a VR game, thereby establishing an organic study environment. Through qualitative analysis, we uncover the interplay between participants' interaction experiences and their security perception. Remarkably, despite encountering unique challenges in usability during VR interactions, our participants found the intuitive virtualized authentication process beneficial and thoroughly enjoyed the immersive nature of VR. Furthermore, we observe how these interaction experiences influence participants' ability to transfer their pre-existing understanding of authentication into VR, resulting in a discrepancy in perceived security. Moreover, we identify users' conflicting expectations, encompassing their desire for an enjoyable VR experience alongside the assurance of secure VR authentication. Building upon our findings, we propose recommendations aimed at addressing these expectations and alleviating potential conflicts.
abstract_zh: 用户很容易在游戏、社交互动、购物和商务等各种日常环境中接受虚拟现实 (VR) 技术的快速发展。为了促进交易和支付，VR系统需要访问敏感的用户数据和资产，因此需要用户身份验证。然而，对于用户在 VR 中的独特体验如何影响他们的安全感知，人们的了解还很有限。在我们的研究中，我们采用了一种称为“技术探索”的研究方法来调查这个问题。具体来说，我们设计了探索 VR 中身份验证过程的探针，旨在从多个角度引出参与者的响应。这些探针无缝集成到VR游戏的常规支付系统中，从而建立了一个有机的学习环境。通过定性分析，我们揭示了参与者的交互体验与其安全感知之间的相互作用。值得注意的是，尽管在 VR 交互过程中遇到了可用性方面的独特挑战，但我们的参与者发现直观的虚拟化身份验证过程非常有益，并充分享受 VR 的沉浸式本质。此外，我们观察这些交互体验如何影响参与者将他们先前对身份验证的理解转移到 VR 中的能力，从而导致感知安全性的差异。此外，我们还确定了用户相互冲突的期望，包括他们对愉快的 VR 体验的渴望以及确保安全的 VR 身份验证的愿望。根据我们的发现，我们提出旨在满足这些期望并减轻潜在冲突的建议。

AI.tldr: 本文研究虚拟现实（VR）中的交互如何影响用户对VR认证安全性的感知。
AI.motivation: 随着VR技术在日常场景中的普及，用户在VR中进行交易和支付时需认证，但现有研究缺乏对VR体验如何塑造用户安全感知的理解。
AI.method: 采用‘技术探针’研究方法，设计嵌入VR游戏支付系统的探针，通过定性分析用户交互体验与安全感知的关系。
AI.result: 用户认为直观的虚拟认证流程有益且沉浸感强，但存在将传统认证认知迁移至VR时的感知安全差异，同时存在享受体验与安全需求的冲突预期。
AI.conclusion: 需通过优化交互设计平衡用户对VR体验的享受与安全需求，以缓解认知差异与冲突预期。

authors_text: Girish Narayanswamy,Yujia Liu,Yuzhe Yang,Chengqian Ma,Xin Liu,Daniel McDuff,Shwetak Patel
title: BigSmall: Efficient Multi-Task Learning for Disparate Spatial and Temporal Physiological Measurements
title_zh: BigSmall：针对不同时空生理测量的高效多任务学习
abstract: Understanding of human visual perception has historically inspired the design of computer vision architectures. As an example, perception occurs at different scales both spatially and temporally, suggesting that the extraction of salient visual information may be made more effective by paying attention to specific features at varying scales. Visual changes in the body due to physiological processes also occur at different scales and with modality-specific characteristic properties. Inspired by this, we present BigSmall, an efficient architecture for physiological and behavioral measurement. We present the first joint camera-based facial action, cardiac, and pulmonary measurement model. We propose a multi-branch network with wrapping temporal shift modules that yields both accuracy and efficiency gains. We observe that fusing low-level features leads to suboptimal performance, but that fusing high level features enables efficiency gains with negligible loss in accuracy. Experimental results demonstrate that BigSmall significantly reduces the computational costs. Furthermore, compared to existing task-specific models, BigSmall achieves comparable or better results on multiple physiological measurement tasks simultaneously with a unified model.
summary: Understanding of human visual perception has historically inspired the design of computer vision architectures. As an example, perception occurs at different scales both spatially and temporally, suggesting that the extraction of salient visual information may be made more effective by paying attention to specific features at varying scales. Visual changes in the body due to physiological processes also occur at different scales and with modality-specific characteristic properties. Inspired by this, we present BigSmall, an efficient architecture for physiological and behavioral measurement. We present the first joint camera-based facial action, cardiac, and pulmonary measurement model. We propose a multi-branch network with wrapping temporal shift modules that yields both accuracy and efficiency gains. We observe that fusing low-level features leads to suboptimal performance, but that fusing high level features enables efficiency gains with negligible loss in accuracy. Experimental results demonstrate that BigSmall significantly reduces the computational costs. Furthermore, compared to existing task-specific models, BigSmall achieves comparable or better results on multiple physiological measurement tasks simultaneously with a unified model.
abstract_zh: 对人类视觉感知的理解历来启发了计算机视觉架构的设计。例如，感知在空间和时间上发生在不同的尺度上，这表明通过关注不同尺度的特定特征可以更有效地提取显着的视觉信息。由于生理过程引起的身体视觉变化也会以不同的尺度发生并具有特定形态的特征属性。受此启发，我们推出了 BigSmall，一种用于生理和行为测量的高效架构。我们提出了第一个基于联合摄像头的面部动作、心脏和肺部测量模型。我们提出了一种多分支网络，其中包含时间移位模块，可以提高准确性和效率。我们观察到，融合低级特征会导致性能不佳，但融合高级特征可以提高效率，而准确性损失可以忽略不计。实验结果表明，BigSmall 显着降低了计算成本。此外，与现有的特定任务模型相比，BigSmall 使用统一模型同时在多个生理测量任务上取得了可比较或更好的结果。

AI.tldr: 设计高效多任务学习模型BigSmall，用于处理不同空间和时间尺度的生理信号测量，包括面部动作、心率和呼吸等任务。
AI.motivation: 受人类视觉感知多尺度特性的启发，针对生理信号在空间和时间上的异质性，提出统一模型以兼顾效率与精度。
AI.method: 构建多分支网络结合时间移位模块，通过高层特征融合实现跨任务信息共享，降低计算成本。
AI.result: 实验表明BigSmall显著减少计算量，在面部动作、心率及呼吸测量任务中达到或超越现有模型性能。
AI.conclusion: BigSmall通过多尺度特征融合与高效架构设计，为多模态生理信号测量提供了兼顾精度与计算效率的解决方案。

authors_text: Ya Qiao,Yajun Zhang,Zhe Yuan
title: Nonuniform magnetic domain-wall synapses enabled by population coding
title_zh: 通过群体编码实现非均匀磁畴壁突触
abstract: Traditional artificial intelligence implemented in software is usually executed on accurate digital computers. Nevertheless, the nanoscale devices for the implementation of neuromorphic computing may not be ideally identical, and the performance is reduced by nonuniform devices. In biological brains, information is usually encoded by a cluster of neurons such that the variability of nerve cells does not influence the accuracy of human cognition and movement. Here, we introduce the population encoding strategy in neuromorphic computing and demonstrate that this strategy can overcome the problems caused by nonuniform devices. Using magnetic memristor device based on current-induced domain-wall motion as an example, we show that imperfect storage devices can be applied in a hardware network to perform principal component analysis (PCA), and the accuracy of unsupervised classification is comparable to that of conventional PCA using ideally accurate synaptic weights. Our results pave the way for hardware implementation of neuromorphic computing and lower the criteria for the uniformity of nanoscale devices.
summary: Traditional artificial intelligence implemented in software is usually executed on accurate digital computers. Nevertheless, the nanoscale devices for the implementation of neuromorphic computing may not be ideally identical, and the performance is reduced by nonuniform devices. In biological brains, information is usually encoded by a cluster of neurons such that the variability of nerve cells does not influence the accuracy of human cognition and movement. Here, we introduce the population encoding strategy in neuromorphic computing and demonstrate that this strategy can overcome the problems caused by nonuniform devices. Using magnetic memristor device based on current-induced domain-wall motion as an example, we show that imperfect storage devices can be applied in a hardware network to perform principal component analysis (PCA), and the accuracy of unsupervised classification is comparable to that of conventional PCA using ideally accurate synaptic weights. Our results pave the way for hardware implementation of neuromorphic computing and lower the criteria for the uniformity of nanoscale devices.
abstract_zh: 以软件实现的传统人工智能通常在精确的数字计算机上执行。然而，用于实现神经形态计算的纳米级设备可能并不理想地相同，并且不均匀的设备会降低性能。在生物大脑中，信息通常由一组神经元编码，因此神经细胞的变异性不会影响人类认知和运动的准确性。在这里，我们介绍了神经形态计算中的群体编码策略，并证明该策略可以克服非均匀设备引起的问题。以基于电流感应畴壁运动的磁忆阻器器件为例，我们表明不完美的存储器件可以应用于硬件网络中进行主成分分析（PCA），并且无监督分类的准确性与使用理想精确突触权重的传统PCA相当。我们的结果为神经形态计算的硬件实现铺平了道路，并降低了纳米级设备均匀性的标准。

AI.tldr: 本文提出通过群体编码策略解决神经形态计算中非均匀器件的问题，并利用磁性忆阻器实现主成分分析（PCA）。
AI.motivation: 传统人工智能依赖精确数字计算机，但纳米级器件的非均匀性导致性能下降，而生物大脑通过神经元集群编码克服了细胞变异的影响。
AI.method: 引入群体编码策略，采用基于电流诱导畴壁运动的磁性忆阻器器件，在硬件网络中实现PCA。
AI.result: 使用非理想存储器件的硬件网络完成PCA，其无监督分类准确率与传统理想权重PCA相当。

search_text: Nonuniform magnetic domain-wall synapses enabled by population coding 通过群体编码实现非均匀磁畴壁突触 Traditional artificial intelligence implemented in software is usually executed on accurate digital computers. Nevertheless, the nanoscale devices for the implementation of neuromorphic computing may not be ideally identical, and the performance is reduced by nonuniform devices. In biological brains, information is usually encoded by a cluster of neurons such that the variability of nerve cells does not influence the accuracy of human cognition and movement. Here, we introduce the population encoding strategy in neuromorphic computing and demonstrate that this strategy can overcome the problems caused by nonuniform devices. Using magnetic memristor device based on current-induced domain-wall motion as an example, we show that imperfect storage devices can be applied in a hardware network to perform principal component analysis (PCA), and the accuracy of unsupervised classification is comparable to that of conventional PCA using ideally accurate synaptic weights. Our results pave the way for hardware implementation of neuromorphic computing and lower the criteria for the uniformity of nanoscale devices. 以软件实现的传统人工智能通常在精确的数字计算机上执行。然而，用于实现神经形态计算的纳米级设备可能并不理想地相同，并且不均匀的设备会降低性能。在生物大脑中，信息通常由一组神经元编码，因此神经细胞的变异性不会影响人类认知和运动的准确性。在这里，我们介绍了神经形态计算中的群体编码策略，并证明该策略可以克服非均匀设备引起的问题。以基于电流感应畴壁运动的磁忆阻器器件为例，我们表明不完美的存储器件可以应用于硬件网络中进行主成分分析（PCA），并且无监督分类的准确性与使用理想精确突触权重的传统PCA相当。我们的结果为神经形态计算的硬件实现铺平了道路，并降低了纳米级设备均匀性的标准。 本文提出通过群体编码策略解决神经形态计算中非均匀器件的问题，并利用磁性忆阻器实现主成分分析（PCA）。 Ya Qiao,Yajun Zhang,Zhe Yuan cs.ET,cond-mat.dis-nn,cond-mat.mtrl-sci

authors_text: Min Chen,Weizhuo Gao,Gaoyang Liu,Kai Peng,Chen Wang
title: Boundary Unlearning
title_zh: 边界遗忘
comment: To be published in Proc.of 34th IEEE/CVF CVPR, 2023
comments: To be published in Proc.of 34th IEEE/CVF CVPR, 2023
abstract: The practical needs of the ``right to be forgotten'' and poisoned data removal call for efficient \textit{machine unlearning} techniques, which enable machine learning models to unlearn, or to forget a fraction of training data and its lineage. Recent studies on machine unlearning for deep neural networks (DNNs) attempt to destroy the influence of the forgetting data by scrubbing the model parameters. However, it is prohibitively expensive due to the large dimension of the parameter space. In this paper, we refocus our attention from the parameter space to the decision space of the DNN model, and propose Boundary Unlearning, a rapid yet effective way to unlearn an entire class from a trained DNN model. The key idea is to shift the decision boundary of the original DNN model to imitate the decision behavior of the model retrained from scratch. We develop two novel boundary shift methods, namely Boundary Shrink and Boundary Expanding, both of which can rapidly achieve the utility and privacy guarantees. We extensively evaluate Boundary Unlearning on CIFAR-10 and Vggface2 datasets, and the results show that Boundary Unlearning can effectively forget the forgetting class on image classification and face recognition tasks, with an expected speed-up of $17\times$ and $19\times$, respectively, compared with retraining from the scratch.
summary: The practical needs of the ``right to be forgotten'' and poisoned data removal call for efficient \textit{machine unlearning} techniques, which enable machine learning models to unlearn, or to forget a fraction of training data and its lineage. Recent studies on machine unlearning for deep neural networks (DNNs) attempt to destroy the influence of the forgetting data by scrubbing the model parameters. However, it is prohibitively expensive due to the large dimension of the parameter space. In this paper, we refocus our attention from the parameter space to the decision space of the DNN model, and propose Boundary Unlearning, a rapid yet effective way to unlearn an entire class from a trained DNN model. The key idea is to shift the decision boundary of the original DNN model to imitate the decision behavior of the model retrained from scratch. We develop two novel boundary shift methods, namely Boundary Shrink and Boundary Expanding, both of which can rapidly achieve the utility and privacy guarantees. We extensively evaluate Boundary Unlearning on CIFAR-10 and Vggface2 datasets, and the results show that Boundary Unlearning can effectively forget the forgetting class on image classification and face recognition tasks, with an expected speed-up of $17\times$ and $19\times$, respectively, compared with retraining from the scratch.
abstract_zh: “被遗忘权”和有毒数据删除的实际需求需要有效的 \textit{机器忘却} 技术，该技术使机器学习模型能够忘却或忘记一部分训练数据及其谱系。最近关于深度神经网络（DNN）机器遗忘的研究试图通过清理模型参数来消除遗忘数据的影响。然而，由于参数空间的维数很大，其成本非常昂贵。在本文中，我们将注意力从 DNN 模型的参数空间重新集中到决策空间，并提出了边界取消学习，这是一种快速而有效的方法，可以从经过训练的 DNN 模型中取消学习整个类。关键思想是改变原始 DNN 模型的决策边界，以模仿从头开始重新训练的模型的决策行为。我们开发了两种新颖的边界移动方法，即边界收缩和边界扩展，这两种方法都可以快速实现实用性和隐私保证。我们在 CIFAR-10 和 Vggface2 数据集上广泛评估了边界取消学习，结果表明，边界取消学习可以有效地忘记图像分类和人脸识别任务中的遗忘类，与从头开始重新训练相比，预期加速分别为 $17\times$ 和 $19\times$。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为Boundary Unlearning的方法，通过调整深度神经网络的决策边界，快速有效地从已训练模型中删除特定类别的数据。
AI.motivation: 现有的机器遗忘技术因需修改模型参数而计算成本高昂，难以满足实际中数据删除需求的效率要求。
AI.method: 将注意力从参数空间转向决策空间，提出边界收缩和边界扩展两种方法，通过移动决策边界模拟从头训练模型的遗忘效果。
AI.result: 在CIFAR-10和Vggface2数据集上验证，该方法在图像分类和人脸识别任务中能有效遗忘指定类别，速度分别比从头训练快17倍和19倍。
AI.conclusion: Boundary Unlearning提供了一种高效且实用的机器遗忘解决方案，在保证效用和隐私的同时显著降低计算开销。

authors_text: Jianing Qiu,Lin Li,Jiankai Sun,Jiachuan Peng,Peilun Shi,Ruiyang Zhang,Yinzhao Dong,Kyle Lam,Frank P. -W. Lo,Bo Xiao,Wu Yuan,Ningli Wang,Dong Xu,Benny Lo
title: Large AI Models in Health Informatics: Applications, Challenges, and the Future
title_zh: 健康信息学中的大型人工智能模型：应用、挑战和未来
comment: This article has been accepted for publication in IEEE Journal of Biomedical and Health Informatics
comments: This article has been accepted for publication in IEEE Journal of Biomedical and Health Informatics
abstract: Large AI models, or foundation models, are models recently emerging with massive scales both parameter-wise and data-wise, the magnitudes of which can reach beyond billions. Once pretrained, large AI models demonstrate impressive performance in various downstream tasks. A prime example is ChatGPT, whose capability has compelled people's imagination about the far-reaching influence that large AI models can have and their potential to transform different domains of our lives. In health informatics, the advent of large AI models has brought new paradigms for the design of methodologies. The scale of multi-modal data in the biomedical and health domain has been ever-expanding especially since the community embraced the era of deep learning, which provides the ground to develop, validate, and advance large AI models for breakthroughs in health-related areas. This article presents a comprehensive review of large AI models, from background to their applications. We identify seven key sectors in which large AI models are applicable and might have substantial influence, including 1) bioinformatics; 2) medical diagnosis; 3) medical imaging; 4) medical informatics; 5) medical education; 6) public health; and 7) medical robotics. We examine their challenges, followed by a critical discussion about potential future directions and pitfalls of large AI models in transforming the field of health informatics.
summary: Large AI models, or foundation models, are models recently emerging with massive scales both parameter-wise and data-wise, the magnitudes of which can reach beyond billions. This article presents a comprehensive review of large AI models, from background to their applications. We identify seven key sectors in which large AI models are applicable and might have substantial influence, including 1) bioinformatics; 2) medical diagnosis; 3) medical imaging; 4) medical informatics; 5) medical education; 6) public health; and 7) medical robotics. We examine their challenges, followed by a critical discussion about potential future directions and pitfalls of large AI models in transforming the field of health informatics.
abstract_zh: 大型人工智能模型，或者说基础模型，是最近出现的具有大规模参数和数据规模的模型，其规模可以达到数十亿以上。经过预训练后，大型人工智能模型在各种下游任务中表现出令人印象深刻的性能。一个典型的例子是 ChatGPT，它的能力激发了人们对大型人工智能模型可能产生的深远影响及其改变我们生活不同领域的潜力的想象。在健康信息学领域，大型人工智能模型的出现为方法设计带来了新的范式。生物医学和健康领域的多模态数据规模不断扩大，特别是自从社区进入深度学习时代以来，这为开发、验证和推进大型人工智能模型以实现健康相关领域的突破提供了基础。本文对大型人工智能模型从背景到应用进行了全面回顾。我们确定了大型人工智能模型适用并可能产生重大影响的七个关键领域，包括1）生物信息学； 2）医学诊断； 3）医学影像； 4）医学信息学； 5）医学教育； 6）公共卫生； 7）医疗机器人技术。我们研究了他们面临的挑战，然后对大型人工智能模型在改变健康信息学领域的潜在未来方向和陷阱进行了批判性讨论。

AI.tldr: 本文综述了大型AI模型在健康信息学中的应用、挑战及未来方向。
AI.motivation: 随着大型AI模型的出现，其在健康信息学领域展现出巨大潜力，因此需要系统评估其应用和挑战。
AI.method: 通过文献综述，识别大型AI模型在健康信息学的七大应用领域，并分析其挑战和未来发展方向。
AI.result: 总结了大型AI模型在生物信息学、医学诊断、医学影像等七个领域的应用，并探讨了其面临的挑战与未来研究方向。
AI.conclusion: 大型AI模型在健康信息学中具有广泛前景，但需解决数据隐私、模型可解释性等问题以实现可持续发展。

search_text: Large AI Models in Health Informatics: Applications, Challenges, and the Future 健康信息学中的大型人工智能模型：应用、挑战和未来 Large AI models, or foundation models, are models recently emerging with massive scales both parameter-wise and data-wise, the magnitudes of which can reach beyond billions. Once pretrained, large AI models demonstrate impressive performance in various downstream tasks. A prime example is ChatGPT, whose capability has compelled people's imagination about the far-reaching influence that large AI models can have and their potential to transform different domains of our lives. In health informatics, the advent of large AI models has brought new paradigms for the design of methodologies. The scale of multi-modal data in the biomedical and health domain has been ever-expanding especially since the community embraced the era of deep learning, which provides the ground to develop, validate, and advance large AI models for breakthroughs in health-related areas. This article presents a comprehensive review of large AI models, from background to their applications. We identify seven key sectors in which large AI models are applicable and might have substantial influence, including 1) bioinformatics; 2) medical diagnosis; 3) medical imaging; 4) medical informatics; 5) medical education; 6) public health; and 7) medical robotics. We examine their challenges, followed by a critical discussion about potential future directions and pitfalls of large AI models in transforming the field of health informatics. 大型人工智能模型，或者说基础模型，是最近出现的具有大规模参数和数据规模的模型，其规模可以达到数十亿以上。经过预训练后，大型人工智能模型在各种下游任务中表现出令人印象深刻的性能。一个典型的例子是 ChatGPT，它的能力激发了人们对大型人工智能模型可能产生的深远影响及其改变我们生活不同领域的潜力的想象。在健康信息学领域，大型人工智能模型的出现为方法设计带来了新的范式。生物医学和健康领域的多模态数据规模不断扩大，特别是自从社区进入深度学习时代以来，这为开发、验证和推进大型人工智能模型以实现健康相关领域的突破提供了基础。本文对大型人工智能模型从背景到应用进行了全面回顾。我们确定了大型人工智能模型适用并可能产生重大影响的七个关键领域，包括1）生物信息学； 2）医学诊断； 3）医学影像； 4）医学信息学； 5）医学教育； 6）公共卫生； 7）医疗机器人技术。我们研究了他们面临的挑战，然后对大型人工智能模型在改变健康信息学领域的潜在未来方向和陷阱进行了批判性讨论。 本文综述了大型AI模型在健康信息学中的应用、挑战及未来方向。 Jianing Qiu,Lin Li,Jiankai Sun,Jiachuan Peng,Peilun Shi,Ruiyang Zhang,Yinzhao Dong,Kyle Lam,Frank P. -W. Lo,Bo Xiao,Wu Yuan,Ningli Wang,Dong Xu,Benny Lo cs.AI,cs.CY

authors_text: Shuai Li,Minghan Li,Ruihuang Li,Chenhang He,Lei Zhang
title: One-to-Few Label Assignment for End-to-End Dense Detection
title_zh: 用于端到端密集检测的一对少标签分配
abstract: One-to-one (o2o) label assignment plays a key role for transformer based end-to-end detection, and it has been recently introduced in fully convolutional detectors for end-to-end dense detection. However, o2o can degrade the feature learning efficiency due to the limited number of positive samples. Though extra positive samples are introduced to mitigate this issue in recent DETRs, the computation of self- and cross- attentions in the decoder limits its practical application to dense and fully convolutional detectors. In this work, we propose a simple yet effective one-to-few (o2f) label assignment strategy for end-to-end dense detection. Apart from defining one positive and many negative anchors for each object, we define several soft anchors, which serve as positive and negative samples simultaneously. The positive and negative weights of these soft anchors are dynamically adjusted during training so that they can contribute more to ``representation learning'' in the early training stage, and contribute more to ``duplicated prediction removal'' in the later stage. The detector trained in this way can not only learn a strong feature representation but also perform end-to-end dense detection. Experiments on COCO and CrowdHuman datasets demonstrate the effectiveness of the o2f scheme. Code is available at https://github.com/strongwolf/o2f.
summary: One-to-one (o2o) label assignment plays a key role for transformer based end-to-end detection, and it has been recently introduced in fully convolutional detectors for end-to-end dense detection. However, o2o can degrade the feature learning efficiency due to the limited number of positive samples. Though extra positive samples are introduced to mitigate this issue in recent DETRs, the computation of self- and cross- attentions in the decoder limits its practical application to dense and fully convolutional detectors. In this work, we propose a simple yet effective one-to-few (o2f) label assignment strategy for end-to-end dense detection. Apart from defining one positive and many negative anchors for each object, we define several soft anchors, which serve as positive and negative samples simultaneously. The positive and negative weights of these soft anchors are dynamically adjusted during training so that they can contribute more to ``representation learning'' in the early training stage, and contribute more to ``duplicated prediction removal'' in the later stage. The detector trained in this way can not only learn a strong feature representation but also perform end-to-end dense detection. Experiments on COCO and CrowdHuman datasets demonstrate the effectiveness of the o2f scheme. Code is available at https://github.com/strongwolf/o2f.
abstract_zh: 一对一 (o2o) 标签分配在基于 Transformer 的端到端检测中发挥着关键作用，最近已在全卷积检测器中引入，用于端到端密集检测。然而，由于正样本数量有限，o2o 会降低特征学习效率。尽管在最近的 DETR 中引入了额外的正样本来缓解这个问题，但解码器中自注意力和交叉注意力的计算限制了其在密集和全卷积检测器中的实际应用。在这项工作中，我们提出了一种简单而有效的一对多（o2f）标签分配策略，用于端到端密集检测。除了为每个对象定义一个正锚点和多个负锚点之外，我们还定义了几个软锚点，它们同时充当正样本和负样本。这些软anchor的正负权重在训练过程中动态调整，使其能够在早期训练阶段为“表示学习”做出更多贡献，并在后期为“重复预测去除”做出更多贡献。以这种方式训练的检测器不仅可以学习强大的特征表示，还可以执行端到端的密集检测。在 COCO 和 CrowdHuman 数据集上的实验证明了 o2f 方案的有效性。代码可在 https://github.com/strongwolf/o2f 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种用于端到端密集检测的一对多标签分配策略（o2f），以提高检测性能。
AI.motivation: 现有的端到端检测方法中，一对一（o2o）标签分配策略由于正样本数量有限，限制了特征学习效率，而引入额外正样本的DETR方法在密集检测中存在计算瓶颈。
AI.method: 本文提出了一种简单而有效的o2f标签分配策略，除了定义每个物体的正样本和多个负样本外，还定义了多个软锚点，这些软锚点可以同时作为正负样本，并在训练过程中动态调整其权重，以促进特征学习和减少重复预测。
AI.result: 在COCO和CrowdHuman数据集上的实验表明，o2f策略在端到端密集检测任务中表现有效。
AI.conclusion: o2f标签分配策略可以提升特征表示能力并实现端到端密集检测，具有良好的应用前景。

authors_text: Li Luo,Zhiyi Mi,Junyi Huang,Zhichao Ruan
title: Wavelength-division multiplexing optical Ising simulator enabling fully programmable spin couplings and external magnetic fields
title_zh: 波分复用光学 Ising 模拟器可实现完全可编程的自旋耦合和外部磁场
abstract: Recently, spatial photonic Ising machines (SPIMs) have demonstrated the abilities to compute the Ising Hamiltonian of large-scale spin systems, with the advantages of ultrafast speed and high power efficiency. However, such optical computations have been limited to specific Ising models with fully connected couplings. Here we develop a wavelength-division multiplexing SPIM to enable programmable spin couplings and external magnetic fields as well for general Ising models. We experimentally demonstrate such a wavelength-division multiplexing SPIM with a single spatial light modulator, where the gauge transformation is implemented to eliminate the impact of pixel alignment. To show the programmable capability of general spin coupling interactions, we explore three spin systems: $\pm J$ models, Sherrington-Kirkpatrick models, and only locally connected ${{J}_{1}}\texttt{-}{{J}_{2}}$ models and observe the phase transitions among the spin-glass, the ferromagnetic, the paramagnetic and the stripe-antiferromagnetic phases. These results show that the wavelength-division multiplexing approach has great programmable flexibility of spin couplings and external magnetic fields, which provides the opportunities to solve general combinatorial optimization problems with large-scale and on-demand SPIM.
summary: Recently, spatial photonic Ising machines (SPIMs) have demonstrated the abilities to compute the Ising Hamiltonian of large-scale spin systems, with the advantages of ultrafast speed and high power efficiency. However, such optical computations have been limited to specific Ising models with fully connected couplings. Here we develop a wavelength-division multiplexing SPIM to enable programmable spin couplings and external magnetic fields as well for general Ising models. We experimentally demonstrate such a wavelength-division multiplexing SPIM with a single spatial light modulator, where the gauge transformation is implemented to eliminate the impact of pixel alignment. To show the programmable capability of general spin coupling interactions, we explore three spin systems: $\pm J$ models, Sherrington-Kirkpatrick models, and only locally connected ${{J}_{1}}\texttt{-}{{J}_{2}}$ models and observe the phase transitions among the spin-glass, the ferromagnetic, the paramagnetic and the stripe-antiferromagnetic phases. These results show that the wavelength-division multiplexing approach has great programmable flexibility of spin couplings and external magnetic fields, which provides the opportunities to solve general combinatorial optimization problems with large-scale and on-demand SPIM.
abstract_zh: 最近，空间光子伊辛机（SPIM）已经证明了计算大规模自旋系统伊辛哈密顿量的能力，具有超快的速度和高功率效率的优势。然而，此类光学计算仅限于具有全连接耦合的特定伊辛模型。在这里，我们开发了一种波分复用 SPIM，以实现可编程自旋耦合和外部磁场以及通用 Ising 模型。我们通过实验证明了这种具有单个空间光调制器的波分复用 SPIM，其中实施规范变换以消除像素对齐的影响。为了展示一般自旋耦合相互作用的可编程能力，我们探索了三种自旋系统：$\pm J$模型、Sherrington-Kirkpatrick模型和仅局部连接的${{J}_{1}}\texttt{-}{{J}_{2}}$模型，并观察自旋玻璃、铁磁、顺磁和条状反铁磁相之间的相变。这些结果表明，波分复用方法具有很大的自旋耦合和外部磁场的可编程灵活性，这为解决大规模和按需 SPIM 的一般组合优化问题提供了机会。

AI.tldr: 开发一种基于波分复用技术的可编程光子Ising机，实现通用自旋耦合与外部磁场调控，解决大规模组合优化问题。
AI.motivation: 现有光子Ising机仅能处理特定全连接耦合模型，缺乏对通用Ising模型及可编程性的支持，限制了实际应用。
AI.method: 设计波分复用空间光子Ising机，通过单空间光调制器与规范变换消除像素对齐误差，实现自旋耦合与磁场的全编程控制。
AI.result: 实验验证了±J模型、Sherrington-Kirkpatrick模型及J1-J2模型的相变特性，证明系统可编程性与处理大规模问题的能力。
AI.conclusion: 该方法显著提升了光子Ising机的灵活性与通用性，为解决复杂组合优化问题提供了高效硬件平台。

We experimentally demonstrate such a wavelength-division multiplexing SPIM with a single spatial light modulator, where the gauge transformation is implemented to eliminate the impact of pixel alignment. These results show that the wavelength-division multiplexing approach has great programmable flexibility of spin couplings and external magnetic fields, which provides the opportunities to solve general combinatorial optimization problems with large-scale and on-demand SPIM. 最近，空间光子伊辛机（SPIM）已经证明了计算大规模自旋系统伊辛哈密顿量的能力，具有超快的速度和高功率效率的优势。然而，此类光学计算仅限于具有全连接耦合的特定伊辛模型。在这里，我们开发了一种波分复用 SPIM，以实现可编程自旋耦合和外部磁场以及通用 Ising 模型。我们通过实验证明了这种具有单个空间光调制器的波分复用 SPIM，其中实施规范变换以消除像素对齐的影响。为了展示一般自旋耦合相互作用的可编程能力，我们探索了三种自旋系统：$\pm J$模型、Sherrington-Kirkpatrick模型和仅局部连接的${{J}_{1}}\texttt{-}{{J}_{2}}$模型，并观察自旋玻璃、铁磁、顺磁和条状反铁磁相之间的相变。这些结果表明，波分复用方法具有很大的自旋耦合和外部磁场的可编程灵活性，这为解决大规模和按需 SPIM 的一般组合优化问题提供了机会。 开发一种基于波分复用技术的可编程光子Ising机，实现通用自旋耦合与外部磁场调控，解决大规模组合优化问题。 Li Luo,Zhiyi Mi,Junyi Huang,Zhichao Ruan physics.optics,cond-mat.stat-mech,cs.ET,physics.app-ph

authors_text: Abraham Sánchez,Raúl Nanclares,Alexander Quevedo,Ulises Pelagio,Alejandra Aguilar,Gabriela Calvario,E. Ulises Moya-Sánchez
title: Agave crop segmentation and maturity classification with deep learning data-centric strategies using very high-resolution satellite imagery
title_zh: 使用超高分辨率卫星图像，通过以数据为中心的深度学习策略进行龙舌兰作物分割和成熟度分类
abstract: The responsible and sustainable agave-tequila production chain is fundamental for the social, environment and economic development of Mexico's agave regions. It is therefore relevant to develop new tools for large scale automatic agave region monitoring. In this work, we present an Agave tequilana Weber azul crop segmentation and maturity classification using very high resolution satellite imagery, which could be useful for this task. To achieve this, we solve real-world deep learning problems in the very specific context of agave crop segmentation such as lack of data, low quality labels, highly imbalanced data, and low model performance. The proposed strategies go beyond data augmentation and data transfer combining active learning and the creation of synthetic images with human supervision. As a result, the segmentation performance evaluated with Intersection over Union (IoU) value increased from 0.72 to 0.90 in the test set. We also propose a method for classifying agave crop maturity with 95% accuracy. With the resulting accurate models, agave production forecasting can be made available for large regions. In addition, some supply-demand problems such excessive supplies of agave or, deforestation, could be detected early.
summary: The responsible and sustainable agave-tequila production chain is fundamental for the social, environment and economic development of Mexico's agave regions. It is therefore relevant to develop new tools for large scale automatic agave region monitoring. In this work, we present an Agave tequilana Weber azul crop segmentation and maturity classification using very high resolution satellite imagery, which could be useful for this task. To achieve this, we solve real-world deep learning problems in the very specific context of agave crop segmentation such as lack of data, low quality labels, highly imbalanced data, and low model performance. The proposed strategies go beyond data augmentation and data transfer combining active learning and the creation of synthetic images with human supervision. As a result, the segmentation performance evaluated with Intersection over Union (IoU) value increased from 0.72 to 0.90 in the test set. We also propose a method for classifying agave crop maturity with 95% accuracy. With the resulting accurate models, agave production forecasting can be made available for large regions. In addition, some supply-demand problems such excessive supplies of agave or, deforestation, could be detected early.
abstract_zh: 负责任且可持续的龙舌兰龙舌兰酒生产链对于墨西哥龙舌兰地区的社会、环境和经济发展至关重要。因此，开发用于大规模自动龙舌兰区域监测的新工具具有重要意义。在这项工作中，我们使用非常高分辨率的卫星图像提出龙舌兰龙舌兰韦伯阿祖尔作物分割和成熟度分类，这对于这项任务可能有用。为了实现这一目标，我们在龙舌兰作物分割的非常具体的背景下解决现实世界的深度学习问题，例如缺乏数据、低质量标签、高度不平衡的数据和低模型性能。所提出的策略超越了数据增强和数据传输，将主动学习和在人类监督下创建合成图像相结合。结果，测试集中用交并比（IoU）值评估的分割性能从 0.72 增加到 0.90。我们还提出了一种对龙舌兰作物成熟度进行分类的方法，准确度为 95%。借助由此产生的准确模型，可以为大面积地区提供龙舌兰产量预测。此外，一些供需问题，例如龙舌兰供应过剩或森林砍伐，可以及早发现。

AI.tldr: 本论文提出利用高分辨率卫星图像结合深度学习方法，实现龙舌兰作物的分割与成熟度分类。
AI.motivation: 龙舌兰-特基拉产业链对墨西哥社会、环境和经济至关重要，需开发大规模自动化监测工具以推动可持续发展并解决供需失衡及滥伐问题。
AI.method: 采用数据增强与迁移学习外的策略，结合主动学习及人工监督的合成图像生成技术，解决数据不足、标签质量低及类别不平衡等挑战。
AI.result: 分割性能（IoU）从0.72提升至0.90，成熟度分类准确率达95%，支持大范围产量预测及供需问题早期预警。
AI.conclusion: 所提方法可有效提升龙舌兰种植监测精度，助力实现可持续生产管理与资源优化配置。

authors_text: Hankyu Jang,Sulyun Lee,D. M. Hasibul Hasan,Philip M. Polgreen,Sriram V. Pemmaraju,Bijaya Adhikari
title: Dynamic Healthcare Embeddings for Improving Patient Care
title_zh: 用于改善患者护理的动态医疗保​​健嵌入
comment: To be published in IEEE/ACM ASONAM 2022
comments: To be published in IEEE/ACM ASONAM 2022
abstract: As hospitals move towards automating and integrating their computing systems, more fine-grained hospital operations data are becoming available. These data include hospital architectural drawings, logs of interactions between patients and healthcare professionals, prescription data, procedures data, and data on patient admission, discharge, and transfers. This has opened up many fascinating avenues for healthcare-related prediction tasks for improving patient care. However, in order to leverage off-the-shelf machine learning software for these tasks, one needs to learn structured representations of entities involved from heterogeneous, dynamic data streams. Here, we propose DECENT, an auto-encoding heterogeneous co-evolving dynamic neural network, for learning heterogeneous dynamic embeddings of patients, doctors, rooms, and medications from diverse data streams. These embeddings capture similarities among doctors, rooms, patients, and medications based on static attributes and dynamic interactions. DECENT enables several applications in healthcare prediction, such as predicting mortality risk and case severity of patients, adverse events (e.g., transfer back into an intensive care unit), and future healthcare-associated infections. The results of using the learned patient embeddings in predictive modeling show that DECENT has a gain of up to 48.1% on the mortality risk prediction task, 12.6% on the case severity prediction task, 6.4% on the medical intensive care unit transfer task, and 3.8% on the Clostridioides difficile (C.diff) Infection (CDI) prediction task over the state-of-the-art baselines. In addition, case studies on the learned doctor, medication, and room embeddings show that our approach learns meaningful and interpretable embeddings.
summary: As hospitals move towards automating and integrating their computing systems, more fine-grained hospital operations data are becoming available. These data include hospital architectural drawings, logs of interactions between patients and healthcare professionals, prescription data, procedures data, and data on patient admission, discharge, and transfers. This has opened up many fascinating avenues for healthcare-related prediction tasks for improving patient care. However, in order to leverage off-the-shelf machine learning software for these tasks, one needs to learn structured representations of entities involved from heterogeneous, dynamic data streams. Here, we propose DECENT, an auto-encoding heterogeneous co-evolving dynamic neural network, for learning heterogeneous dynamic embeddings of patients, doctors, rooms, and medications from diverse data streams. These embeddings capture similarities among doctors, rooms, patients, and medications based on static attributes and dynamic interactions. DECENT enables several applications in healthcare prediction, such as predicting mortality risk and case severity of patients, adverse events (e.g., transfer back into an intensive care unit), and future healthcare-associated infections. The results of using the learned patient embeddings in predictive modeling show that DECENT has a gain of up to 48.1% on the mortality risk prediction task, 12.6% on the case severity prediction task, 6.4% on the medical intensive care unit transfer task, and 3.8% on the Clostridioides difficile (C.diff) Infection (CDI) prediction task over the state-of-the-art baselines. In addition, case studies on the learned doctor, medication, and room embeddings show that our approach learns meaningful and interpretable embeddings.
abstract_zh: 随着医院朝着自动化和集成其计算系统的方向发展，越来越细粒度的医院运营数据变得可用。这些数据包括医院建筑图纸、患者与医疗保健专业人员之间的互动日志、处方数据、程序数据以及患者入院、出院和转移的数据。这为医疗保健相关预测任务开辟了许多令人着迷的途径，以改善患者护理。然而，为了利用现成的机器学习软件来完成这些任务，需要学习异构动态数据流中涉及的实体的结构化表示。在这里，我们提出了 DECENT，一种自动编码异构共同进化动态神经网络，用于从不同的数据流中学习患者、医生、房间和药物的异构动态嵌入。这些嵌入基于静态属性和动态交互来捕获医生、房间、患者和药物之间的相似性。 DECENT 可在医疗保健预测中实现多种应用，例如预测患者的死亡风险和病例严重程度、不良事件（例如转回重症监护病房）以及未来与医疗保健相关的感染。在预测模型中使用学习到的患者嵌入的结果表明，与最先进的基线相比，DECENT 在死亡风险预测任务上的增益高达 48.1%，在病例严重程度预测任务上的增益高达 12.6%，在医疗重症监护病房转移任务上的增益高达 6.4%，在艰难梭菌 (C.diff) 感染 (CDI) 预测任务上的增益高达 3.8%。此外，对博学的医生、药物和房间嵌入的案例研究表明，我们的方法学习了有意义且可解释的嵌入。

AI.tldr: 本文提出DECENT方法，通过动态嵌入技术整合医院多源异构数据，以提升患者护理预测任务的性能。
AI.motivation: 医院系统产生的细粒度操作数据（如患者交互日志、处方数据等）为医疗预测提供了新机会，但现有机器学习工具难以直接处理异构动态数据，需构建结构化实体表示。
AI.method: 设计异构共演化动态神经网络DECENT，通过自编码框架从医院建筑、医患互动、药品等多源数据流中学习患者、医生、房间和药品的动态嵌入表示，捕捉静态属性与动态交互的相似性。
AI.result: 在死亡风险预测任务中优于基线48.1%，重症监护室转科任务提升6.4%，艰难梭菌感染预测提升3.8%，且医生/药品/房间嵌入具有可解释性。
AI.conclusion: DECENT通过动态嵌入有效整合医疗异构数据，在多类预测任务中显著超越现有方法，且生成的嵌入具有临床可解释性，为智能医疗决策提供新工具。

Here, we propose DECENT, an auto-encoding heterogeneous co-evolving dynamic neural network, for learning heterogeneous dynamic embeddings of patients, doctors, rooms, and medications from diverse data streams. These embeddings capture similarities among doctors, rooms, patients, and medications based on static attributes and dynamic interactions. DECENT enables several applications in healthcare prediction, such as predicting mortality risk and case severity of patients, adverse events (e.g., transfer back into an intensive care unit), and future healthcare-associated infections. The results of using the learned patient embeddings in predictive modeling show that DECENT has a gain of up to 48.1% on the mortality risk prediction task, 12.6% on the case severity prediction task, 6.4% on the medical intensive care unit transfer task, and 3.8% on the Clostridioides difficile (C.diff) Infection (CDI) prediction task over the state-of-the-art baselines. In addition, case studies on the learned doctor, medication, and room embeddings show that our approach learns meaningful and interpretable embeddings. 随着医院朝着自动化和集成其计算系统的方向发展，越来越细粒度的医院运营数据变得可用。这些数据包括医院建筑图纸、患者与医疗保健专业人员之间的互动日志、处方数据、程序数据以及患者入院、出院和转移的数据。这为医疗保健相关预测任务开辟了许多令人着迷的途径，以改善患者护理。然而，为了利用现成的机器学习软件来完成这些任务，需要学习异构动态数据流中涉及的实体的结构化表示。在这里，我们提出了 DECENT，一种自动编码异构共同进化动态神经网络，用于从不同的数据流中学习患者、医生、房间和药物的异构动态嵌入。这些嵌入基于静态属性和动态交互来捕获医生、房间、患者和药物之间的相似性。 DECENT 可在医疗保健预测中实现多种应用，例如预测患者的死亡风险和病例严重程度、不良事件（例如转回重症监护病房）以及未来与医疗保健相关的感染。在预测模型中使用学习到的患者嵌入的结果表明，与最先进的基线相比，DECENT 在死亡风险预测任务上的增益高达 48.1%，在病例严重程度预测任务上的增益高达 12.6%，在医疗重症监护病房转移任务上的增益高达 6.4%，在艰难梭菌 (C.diff) 感染 (CDI) 预测任务上的增益高达 3.8%。此外，对博学的医生、药物和房间嵌入的案例研究表明，我们的方法学习了有意义且可解释的嵌入。 本文提出DECENT方法，通过动态嵌入技术整合医院多源异构数据，以提升患者护理预测任务的性能。 Hankyu Jang,Sulyun Lee,D. M. Hasibul Hasan,Philip M. Polgreen,Sriram V. Pemmaraju,Bijaya Adhikari cs.LG,cs.AI,q-bio.QM

authors_text: Harry Dobbs,Oliver Batchelor,Richard Green,James Atlas
title: Smart-Tree: Neural Medial Axis Approximation of Point Clouds for 3D Tree Skeletonization
title_zh: Smart-Tree：用于 3D 树骨架化的点云神经中轴近似
abstract: This paper introduces Smart-Tree, a supervised method for approximating the medial axes of branch skeletons from a tree point cloud. Smart-Tree uses a sparse voxel convolutional neural network to extract the radius and direction towards the medial axis of each input point. A greedy algorithm performs robust skeletonization using the estimated medial axis. Our proposed method provides robustness to complex tree structures and improves fidelity when dealing with self-occlusions, complex geometry, touching branches, and varying point densities. We evaluate Smart-Tree using a multi-species synthetic tree dataset and perform qualitative analysis on a real-world tree point cloud. Our experimentation with synthetic and real-world datasets demonstrates the robustness of our approach over the current state-of-the-art method. The dataset and source code are publicly available.
summary: This paper introduces Smart-Tree, a supervised method for approximating the medial axes of branch skeletons from a tree point cloud. Smart-Tree uses a sparse voxel convolutional neural network to extract the radius and direction towards the medial axis of each input point. A greedy algorithm performs robust skeletonization using the estimated medial axis. Our proposed method provides robustness to complex tree structures and improves fidelity when dealing with self-occlusions, complex geometry, touching branches, and varying point densities. We evaluate Smart-Tree using a multi-species synthetic tree dataset and perform qualitative analysis on a real-world tree point cloud. Our experimentation with synthetic and real-world datasets demonstrates the robustness of our approach over the current state-of-the-art method. The dataset and source code are publicly available.
abstract_zh: 本文介绍了 Smart-Tree，这是一种从树点云近似分支骨架中轴的监督方法。 Smart-Tree 使用稀疏体素卷积神经网络来提取每个输入点的半径和朝向中轴的方向。贪心算法使用估计的中轴执行稳健的骨架化。我们提出的方法为复杂的树结构提供了鲁棒性，并在处理自遮挡、复杂的几何形状、接触分支和变化的点密度时提高了保真度。我们使用多物种合成树数据集评估 Smart-Tree，并对现实世界的树点云进行定性分析。我们对合成数据集和真实数据集的实验证明了我们的方法相对于当前最先进的方法的稳健性。数据集和源代码是公开的。

AI.tldr: 本文提出Smart-Tree方法，利用神经网络对点云进行3D树骨架化，近似计算树枝的中轴线。
AI.motivation: 现有树骨架化方法在处理复杂结构、自遮挡、分支接触及点云密度变化时存在鲁棒性不足的问题。
AI.method: 采用监督学习框架，结合稀疏体素卷积神经网络提取点云中轴线半径和方向，并通过贪心算法实现骨架化。
AI.result: 在多物种合成数据集和真实点云上验证，证明该方法在复杂几何和遮挡场景下优于现有最佳方法。
AI.conclusion: Smart-Tree通过神经网络与贪心算法结合，有效提升了树骨架化的鲁棒性和精度，代码和数据集已开源。

authors_text: Zhaodi Zhang,Zhiyi Xue,Yang Chen,Si Liu,Yueling Zhang,Jing Liu,Min Zhang
title: Boosting Verified Training for Robust Image Classifications via Abstraction
title_zh: 通过抽象促进鲁棒图像分类的验证训练
abstract: This paper proposes a novel, abstraction-based, certified training method for robust image classifiers. Via abstraction, all perturbed images are mapped into intervals before feeding into neural networks for training. By training on intervals, all the perturbed images that are mapped to the same interval are classified as the same label, rendering the variance of training sets to be small and the loss landscape of the models to be smooth. Consequently, our approach significantly improves the robustness of trained models. For the abstraction, our training method also enables a sound and complete black-box verification approach, which is orthogonal and scalable to arbitrary types of neural networks regardless of their sizes and architectures. We evaluate our method on a wide range of benchmarks in different scales. The experimental results show that our method outperforms state of the art by (i) reducing the verified errors of trained models up to 95.64%; (ii) totally achieving up to 602.50x speedup; and (iii) scaling up to larger models with up to 138 million trainable parameters. The demo is available at https://github.com/zhangzhaodi233/ABSCERT.git.
summary: This paper proposes a novel, abstraction-based, certified training method for robust image classifiers. Via abstraction, all perturbed images are mapped into intervals before feeding into neural networks for training. By training on intervals, all the perturbed images that are mapped to the same interval are classified as the same label, rendering the variance of training sets to be small and the loss landscape of the models to be smooth. Consequently, our approach significantly improves the robustness of trained models. For the abstraction, our training method also enables a sound and complete black-box verification approach, which is orthogonal and scalable to arbitrary types of neural networks regardless of their sizes and architectures. We evaluate our method on a wide range of benchmarks in different scales. The experimental results show that our method outperforms state of the art by (i) reducing the verified errors of trained models up to 95.64%; (ii) totally achieving up to 602.50x speedup; and (iii) scaling up to larger models with up to 138 million trainable parameters. The demo is available at https://github.com/zhangzhaodi233/ABSCERT.git.
abstract_zh: 本文提出了一种新颖的、基于抽象的、经过认证的鲁棒图像分类器训练方法。通过抽象，所有扰动图像在输入神经网络进行训练之前都被映射到区间。通过间隔训练，映射到同一间隔的所有扰动图像都被分类为相同的标签，使得训练集的方差很小并且模型的损失景观变得平滑。因此，我们的方法显着提高了训练模型的稳健性。对于抽象，我们的训练方法还支持健全且完整的黑盒验证方法，该方法是正交的并且可扩展至任意类型的神经网络，无论其大小和架构如何。我们在不同规模的广泛基准上评估我们的方法。实验结果表明，我们的方法通过以下方式优于现有技术：(i) 将训练模型的验证错误减少高达 95.64%； (ii) 总共实现高达 602.50 倍的加速； (iii) 扩展到具有多达 1.38 亿个可训练参数的更大模型。该演示可在 https://github.com/zhangzhaodi233/ABSCERT.git 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于抽象的认证训练方法，用于提升图像分类模型的鲁棒性。
AI.motivation: 现有的图像分类模型在面对对抗性攻击时表现不佳，因此需要一种能够提升模型鲁棒性的训练方法。
AI.method: 通过将所有扰动图像映射到区间，再输入神经网络进行训练。这种方法使得映射到同一区间的扰动图像被分类为相同标签，从而减少训练集的方差并使模型的损失景观更加平滑。
AI.result: 实验结果表明，该方法在减少验证错误、提升训练速度和扩展到更大模型方面均优于现有方法。
AI.conclusion: 该方法不仅显著提高了模型的鲁棒性，还提供了一种适用于各种神经网络的黑盒验证方法。

authors_text: Akash Gupta,Rohun Tripathi,Wondong Jang
title: ModEFormer: Modality-Preserving Embedding for Audio-Video Synchronization using Transformers
title_zh: ModEFormer：使用 Transformer 进行音视频同步的模态保留嵌入
abstract: Lack of audio-video synchronization is a common problem during television broadcasts and video conferencing, leading to an unsatisfactory viewing experience. A widely accepted paradigm is to create an error detection mechanism that identifies the cases when audio is leading or lagging. We propose ModEFormer, which independently extracts audio and video embeddings using modality-specific transformers. Different from the other transformer-based approaches, ModEFormer preserves the modality of the input streams which allows us to use a larger batch size with more negative audio samples for contrastive learning. Further, we propose a trade-off between the number of negative samples and number of unique samples in a batch to significantly exceed the performance of previous methods. Experimental results show that ModEFormer achieves state-of-the-art performance, 94.5% for LRS2 and 90.9% for LRS3. Finally, we demonstrate how ModEFormer can be used for offset detection for test clips.
summary: Lack of audio-video synchronization is a common problem during television broadcasts and video conferencing, leading to an unsatisfactory viewing experience. A widely accepted paradigm is to create an error detection mechanism that identifies the cases when audio is leading or lagging. We propose ModEFormer, which independently extracts audio and video embeddings using modality-specific transformers. Different from the other transformer-based approaches, ModEFormer preserves the modality of the input streams which allows us to use a larger batch size with more negative audio samples for contrastive learning. Further, we propose a trade-off between the number of negative samples and number of unique samples in a batch to significantly exceed the performance of previous methods. Experimental results show that ModEFormer achieves state-of-the-art performance, 94.5% for LRS2 and 90.9% for LRS3. Finally, we demonstrate how ModEFormer can be used for offset detection for test clips.
abstract_zh: 音视频不同步是电视广播和视频会议中常见的问题，导致观看体验不佳。一种广泛接受的范例是创建一种错误检测机制，用于识别音频超前或滞后的情况。我们提出了 ModEFormer，它使用特定于模态的转换器独立提取音频和视频嵌入。与其他基于 Transformer 的方法不同，ModEFormer 保留了输入流的模态，这允许我们使用更大的批量大小和更多的负音频样本进行对比学习。此外，我们建议在批次中的负样本数量和唯一样本数量之间进行权衡，以显着超过以前方法的性能。实验结果表明，ModEFormer 实现了最先进的性能，LRS2 为 94.5%，LRS3 为 90.9%。最后，我们演示如何使用 ModEFormer 进行测试剪辑的偏移检测。

AI.tldr: 本文提出ModEFormer模型，利用Transformer独立提取音频和视频嵌入，解决音视频同步问题。
AI.motivation: 音视频不同步影响观看体验，现有方法在处理负样本和批次大小时存在局限，需改进对比学习效率。
AI.method: 采用模态特定Transformer提取音频和视频嵌入，保留输入流模态性，通过负样本与唯一样本的平衡策略优化对比学习。
AI.result: 在LRS2数据集上达到94.5%，LRS3上达90.9%，优于现有方法，并实现测试片段偏移检测。
AI.conclusion: ModEFormer通过模态保留和样本策略优化，有效提升音视频同步检测性能，适用于实际场景的偏移分析。

search_text: ModEFormer: Modality-Preserving Embedding for Audio-Video Synchronization using Transformers ModEFormer：使用 Transformer 进行音视频同步的模态保留嵌入 Lack of audio-video synchronization is a common problem during television broadcasts and video conferencing, leading to an unsatisfactory viewing experience. A widely accepted paradigm is to create an error detection mechanism that identifies the cases when audio is leading or lagging. We propose ModEFormer, which independently extracts audio and video embeddings using modality-specific transformers. Different from the other transformer-based approaches, ModEFormer preserves the modality of the input streams which allows us to use a larger batch size with more negative audio samples for contrastive learning. Further, we propose a trade-off between the number of negative samples and number of unique samples in a batch to significantly exceed the performance of previous methods. Experimental results show that ModEFormer achieves state-of-the-art performance, 94.5% for LRS2 and 90.9% for LRS3. Finally, we demonstrate how ModEFormer can be used for offset detection for test clips. 音视频不同步是电视广播和视频会议中常见的问题，导致观看体验不佳。一种广泛接受的范例是创建一种错误检测机制，用于识别音频超前或滞后的情况。我们提出了 ModEFormer，它使用特定于模态的转换器独立提取音频和视频嵌入。与其他基于 Transformer 的方法不同，ModEFormer 保留了输入流的模态，这允许我们使用更大的批量大小和更多的负音频样本进行对比学习。此外，我们建议在批次中的负样本数量和唯一样本数量之间进行权衡，以显着超过以前方法的性能。实验结果表明，ModEFormer 实现了最先进的性能，LRS2 为 94.5%，LRS3 为 90.9%。最后，我们演示如何使用 ModEFormer 进行测试剪辑的偏移检测。 本文提出ModEFormer模型，利用Transformer独立提取音频和视频嵌入，解决音视频同步问题。 Akash Gupta,Rohun Tripathi,Wondong Jang cs.CV,cs.LG,cs.SD,eess.AS,eess.IV

authors_text: Sahil Goyal,Shagun Uppal,Sarthak Bhagat,Yi Yu,Yifang Yin,Rajiv Ratn Shah
title: Emotionally Enhanced Talking Face Generation
title_zh: 情感增强的说话面孔生成
abstract: Several works have developed end-to-end pipelines for generating lip-synced talking faces with various real-world applications, such as teaching and language translation in videos. However, these prior works fail to create realistic-looking videos since they focus little on people's expressions and emotions. Moreover, these methods' effectiveness largely depends on the faces in the training dataset, which means they may not perform well on unseen faces. To mitigate this, we build a talking face generation framework conditioned on a categorical emotion to generate videos with appropriate expressions, making them more realistic and convincing. With a broad range of six emotions, i.e., \emph{happiness}, \emph{sadness}, \emph{fear}, \emph{anger}, \emph{disgust}, and \emph{neutral}, we show that our model can adapt to arbitrary identities, emotions, and languages. Our proposed framework is equipped with a user-friendly web interface with a real-time experience for talking face generation with emotions. We also conduct a user study for subjective evaluation of our interface's usability, design, and functionality. Project page: https://midas.iiitd.edu.in/emo/
summary: Several works have developed end-to-end pipelines for generating lip-synced talking faces with various real-world applications, such as teaching and language translation in videos. However, these prior works fail to create realistic-looking videos since they focus little on people's expressions and emotions. Moreover, these methods' effectiveness largely depends on the faces in the training dataset, which means they may not perform well on unseen faces. To mitigate this, we build a talking face generation framework conditioned on a categorical emotion to generate videos with appropriate expressions, making them more realistic and convincing. With a broad range of six emotions, i.e., \emph{happiness}, \emph{sadness}, \emph{fear}, \emph{anger}, \emph{disgust}, and \emph{neutral}, we show that our model can adapt to arbitrary identities, emotions, and languages. Our proposed framework is equipped with a user-friendly web interface with a real-time experience for talking face generation with emotions. We also conduct a user study for subjective evaluation of our interface's usability, design, and functionality. Project page: https://midas.iiitd.edu.in/emo/
abstract_zh: 有几项工作已经开发了端到端的管道，用于通过各种现实世界的应用（例如视频中的教学和语言翻译）生成口型同步的说话面孔。然而，这些先前的作品未能创造出逼真的视频，因为它们很少关注人们的表情和情感。此外，这些方法的有效性很大程度上取决于训练数据集中的人脸，这意味着它们可能在看不见的人脸上表现不佳。为了缓解这个问题，我们构建了一个以分类情感为条件的会说话的面部生成框架，以生成具有适当表情的视频，使它们更加真实和令人信服。通过广泛的六种情绪，即 \emph{happiness}、\emph{sadness}、\emph{fear}、\emph{anger}、\emph{disgust} 和 \emph{neutral}，我们证明我们的模型可以适应任意身份、情绪和语言。我们提出的框架配备了一个用户友好的网络界面，可以提供带有情感的面部表情生成的实时体验。我们还进行了用户研究，对我们的界面的可用性、设计和功能进行主观评估。项目页面：https://midas.iiitd.edu.in/emo/

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于情感条件的说话人脸生成框架，通过增强表情和情绪表现提升视频真实感。
AI.motivation: 现有方法生成的唇形同步视频缺乏真实表情和情绪表达，且对未见过的面孔泛化能力差。
AI.method: 构建情感条件生成框架，支持六种情绪类别，并开发实时交互式网页界面进行用户研究。
AI.result: 模型可适配任意身份、情绪和语言，用户研究验证了界面可用性与功能有效性。
AI.conclusion: 情感增强框架显著提升生成视频的真实性，系统具备良好的跨身份泛化能力和用户体验。

authors_text: Dan Hawes,Ali Arya
title: A VR-based Priming Framework and Technology Implementation to Improve Learning Mindsets and Academic Performance in Post-Secondary Students
title_zh: 基于 VR 的启动框架和技术实施，可改善专上学生的学习心态和学业成绩
abstract: Recent research indicates that most post-secondary students in North America "felt overwhelming anxiety" in the past few years, negatively affecting well-being and academic performance. Further research revealed that other emotions, biases, perceptions, and negative thoughts, can similarly affect student academic performance. To address this problem, we classify these counterproductive mindsets, including anxiety, into Scarcity Mindset, a self-limiting perspective that appropriates cognitive bandwidth required for essential processes like learning in favour of addressing more critical needs or perceived insufficiencies. Through a multi-disciplinary literature analysis of ideas in cognitive science, learning theories and mindsets, and current technology approaches that are suited to address the limitations of scarcity thinking, we identify strategies to help transition students to a more positive Abundance Mindsets. We demonstrate that these priming intervention strategies can transfer to leading-edge digital environments, particularly Virtual Reality (VR). Offering further insights into the findings of our two previously presented studies, we argue that priming interventions related to preparatory activities and the context priming are transferable to virtual reality environments. As such, building on our multidisciplinary research insights, we propose a comprehensive priming model that exploits priming techniques in an iterative process called Cyclical Priming Methodology (CPM). These intervention strategies can focus on student preparation, motivation, reflection, the context of the learning environment, and other aspects of the learning process. Building on CPM, we further propose a technology implementation within VR called Virtual Reality Experience Priming (VREP) and discuss the process to embed CPM/VREP activities within the Experiential Learning Theory (ELT) cycle.
summary: Recent research indicates that most post-secondary students in North America "felt overwhelming anxiety" in the past few years, negatively affecting well-being and academic performance. Further research revealed that other emotions, biases, perceptions, and negative thoughts, can similarly affect student academic performance. To address this problem, we classify these counterproductive mindsets, including anxiety, into Scarcity Mindset, a self-limiting perspective that appropriates cognitive bandwidth required for essential processes like learning in favour of addressing more critical needs or perceived insufficiencies. Through a multi-disciplinary literature analysis of ideas in cognitive science, learning theories and mindsets, and current technology approaches that are suited to address the limitations of scarcity thinking, we identify strategies to help transition students to a more positive Abundance Mindsets. We demonstrate that these priming intervention strategies can transfer to leading-edge digital environments, particularly Virtual Reality (VR). Offering further insights into the findings of our two previously presented studies, we argue that priming interventions related to preparatory activities and the context priming are transferable to virtual reality environments. As such, building on our multidisciplinary research insights, we propose a comprehensive priming model that exploits priming techniques in an iterative process called Cyclical Priming Methodology (CPM). These intervention strategies can focus on student preparation, motivation, reflection, the context of the learning environment, and other aspects of the learning process. Building on CPM, we further propose a technology implementation within VR called Virtual Reality Experience Priming (VREP) and discuss the process to embed CPM/VREP activities within the Experiential Learning Theory (ELT) cycle.
abstract_zh: 最近的研究表明，北美大多数专上学生在过去几年中“感到压倒性的焦虑”，这对幸福感和学业成绩产生了负面影响。进一步的研究表明，其他情绪、偏见、看法和消极想法也会同样影响学生的学业成绩。为了解决这个问题，我们将这些适得其反的心态（包括焦虑）归类为稀缺心态，这是一种自我限制的观点，可以合理分配学习等基本过程所需的认知带宽，以支持解决更关键的需求或感知到的不足。通过对认知科学思想、学习理论和思维方式以及适合解决稀缺性思维局限性的当前技术方法进行多学科文献分析，我们确定了帮助学生转变为更积极的丰富思维方式的策略。我们证明这些启动干预策略可以转移到前沿的数字环境，特别是虚拟现实（VR）。对我们之前提出的两项研究的结果提供了进一步的见解，我们认为与准备活动和情境启动相关的启动干预措施可以转移到虚拟现实环境中。因此，基于我们的多学科研究见解，我们提出了一种全面的启动模型，该模型在称为循环启动方法（CPM）的迭代过程中利用启动技术。这些干预策略可以侧重于学生的准备、动机、反思、学习环境的背景以及学习过程的其他方面。在 CPM 的基础上，我们进一步提出了一种称为虚拟现实体验启动 (VREP) 的 VR 技术实现，并讨论了将 CPM/VREP 活动嵌入体验式学习理论 (ELT) 周期的过程。

AI.tldr: 本文提出基于虚拟现实（VR）的‘循环启动方法’（CPM）和‘虚拟现实体验启动’（VREP）技术框架，旨在通过改变大学生的‘稀缺心态’为‘丰裕心态’，提升其学习表现和心理健康。
AI.motivation: 北美大学生普遍面临焦虑等负面情绪，导致学术表现下降。传统方法难以有效干预，需探索基于VR的创新技术手段以重塑学习心态。
AI.method: 通过跨学科文献分析，整合认知科学、学习理论和VR技术，提出CPM模型及VREP技术实现方案，并将其嵌入体验式学习理论（ELT）循环中。
AI.result: 验证了启动干预策略可迁移至VR环境，开发出支持学习准备、动机激发和环境优化的VREP系统，为提升学术表现提供技术路径。
AI.conclusion: VR启动框架能有效缓解学生心理压力，促进积极学习心态形成，为高等教育提供可扩展的沉浸式干预解决方案。

conclusion: VR启动框架能有效缓解学生心理压力，促进积极学习心态形成，为高等教育提供可扩展的沉浸式干预解决方案。
search_text: A VR-based Priming Framework and Technology Implementation to Improve Learning Mindsets and Academic Performance in Post-Secondary Students 基于 VR 的启动框架和技术实施，可改善专上学生的学习心态和学业成绩 Recent research indicates that most post-secondary students in North America "felt overwhelming anxiety" in the past few years, negatively affecting well-being and academic performance. Further research revealed that other emotions, biases, perceptions, and negative thoughts, can similarly affect student academic performance. To address this problem, we classify these counterproductive mindsets, including anxiety, into Scarcity Mindset, a self-limiting perspective that appropriates cognitive bandwidth required for essential processes like learning in favour of addressing more critical needs or perceived insufficiencies. Through a multi-disciplinary literature analysis of ideas in cognitive science, learning theories and mindsets, and current technology approaches that are suited to address the limitations of scarcity thinking, we identify strategies to help transition students to a more positive Abundance Mindsets. We demonstrate that these priming intervention strategies can transfer to leading-edge digital environments, particularly Virtual Reality (VR). Offering further insights into the findings of our two previously presented studies, we argue that priming interventions related to preparatory activities and the context priming are transferable to virtual reality environments. As such, building on our multidisciplinary research insights, we propose a comprehensive priming model that exploits priming techniques in an iterative process called Cyclical Priming Methodology (CPM). These intervention strategies can focus on student preparation, motivation, reflection, the context of the learning environment, and other aspects of the learning process. Building on CPM, we further propose a technology implementation within VR called Virtual Reality Experience Priming (VREP) and discuss the process to embed CPM/VREP activities within the Experiential Learning Theory (ELT) cycle. 最近的研究表明，北美大多数专上学生在过去几年中“感到压倒性的焦虑”，这对幸福感和学业成绩产生了负面影响。进一步的研究表明，其他情绪、偏见、看法和消极想法也会同样影响学生的学业成绩。为了解决这个问题，我们将这些适得其反的心态（包括焦虑）归类为稀缺心态，这是一种自我限制的观点，可以合理分配学习等基本过程所需的认知带宽，以支持解决更关键的需求或感知到的不足。通过对认知科学思想、学习理论和思维方式以及适合解决稀缺性思维局限性的当前技术方法进行多学科文献分析，我们确定了帮助学生转变为更积极的丰富思维方式的策略。我们证明这些启动干预策略可以转移到前沿的数字环境，特别是虚拟现实（VR）。对我们之前提出的两项研究的结果提供了进一步的见解，我们认为与准备活动和情境启动相关的启动干预措施可以转移到虚拟现实环境中。因此，基于我们的多学科研究见解，我们提出了一种全面的启动模型，该模型在称为循环启动方法（CPM）的迭代过程中利用启动技术。这些干预策略可以侧重于学生的准备、动机、反思、学习环境的背景以及学习过程的其他方面。在 CPM 的基础上，我们进一步提出了一种称为虚拟现实体验启动 (VREP) 的 VR 技术实现，并讨论了将 CPM/VREP 活动嵌入体验式学习理论 (ELT) 周期的过程。 本文提出基于虚拟现实（VR）的‘循环启动方法’（CPM）和‘虚拟现实体验启动’（VREP）技术框架，旨在通过改变大学生的‘稀缺心态’为‘丰裕心态’，提升其学习表现和心理健康。 Dan Hawes,Ali Arya cs.HC

authors_text: Sunghwan Kim,Dae-hwan Kim,Hoseong Kim
title: Texture Learning Domain Randomization for Domain Generalized Segmentation
title_zh: 用于域广义分割的纹理学习域随机化
abstract: Deep Neural Networks (DNNs)-based semantic segmentation models trained on a source domain often struggle to generalize to unseen target domains, i.e., a domain gap problem. Texture often contributes to the domain gap, making DNNs vulnerable to domain shift because they are prone to be texture-biased. Existing Domain Generalized Semantic Segmentation (DGSS) methods have alleviated the domain gap problem by guiding models to prioritize shape over texture. On the other hand, shape and texture are two prominent and complementary cues in semantic segmentation. This paper argues that leveraging texture is crucial for improving performance in DGSS. Specifically, we propose a novel framework, coined Texture Learning Domain Randomization (TLDR). TLDR includes two novel losses to effectively enhance texture learning in DGSS: (1) a texture regularization loss to prevent overfitting to source domain textures by using texture features from an ImageNet pre-trained model and (2) a texture generalization loss that utilizes random style images to learn diverse texture representations in a self-supervised manner. Extensive experimental results demonstrate the superiority of the proposed TLDR; e.g., TLDR achieves 46.5 mIoU on GTA-to-Cityscapes using ResNet-50, which improves the prior state-of-the-art method by 1.9 mIoU. The source code is available at https://github.com/ssssshwan/TLDR.
summary: Deep Neural Networks (DNNs)-based semantic segmentation models trained on a source domain often struggle to generalize to unseen target domains, i.e., a domain gap problem. Texture often contributes to the domain gap, making DNNs vulnerable to domain shift because they are prone to be texture-biased. Existing Domain Generalized Semantic Segmentation (DGSS) methods have alleviated the domain gap problem by guiding models to prioritize shape over texture. On the other hand, shape and texture are two prominent and complementary cues in semantic segmentation. This paper argues that leveraging texture is crucial for improving performance in DGSS. Specifically, we propose a novel framework, coined Texture Learning Domain Randomization (TLDR). TLDR includes two novel losses to effectively enhance texture learning in DGSS: (1) a texture regularization loss to prevent overfitting to source domain textures by using texture features from an ImageNet pre-trained model and (2) a texture generalization loss that utilizes random style images to learn diverse texture representations in a self-supervised manner. Extensive experimental results demonstrate the superiority of the proposed TLDR; e.g., TLDR achieves 46.5 mIoU on GTA-to-Cityscapes using ResNet-50, which improves the prior state-of-the-art method by 1.9 mIoU. The source code is available at https://github.com/ssssshwan/TLDR.
abstract_zh: 在源域上训练的基于深度神经网络 (DNN) 的语义分割模型通常很难推广到看不见的目标域，即域间隙问题。纹理通常会造成域间隙，使得 DNN 容易受到域偏移的影响，因为它们容易出现纹理偏差。现有的域广义语义分割（DGSS）方法通过指导模型优先考虑形状而不是纹理，缓解了域间隙问题。另一方面，形状和纹理是语义分割中两个突出且互补的线索。本文认为利用纹理对于提高 DGSS 的性能至关重要。具体来说，我们提出了一个新颖的框架，称为纹理学习域随机化（TLDR）。 TLDR 包括两个新颖的损失，可有效增强 DGSS 中的纹理学习：(1) 纹理正则化损失，通过使用 ImageNet 预训练模型中的纹理特征来防止对源域纹理的过度拟合；(2) 纹理泛化损失，利用随机风格图像以自我监督的方式学习不同的纹理表示。大量的实验结果证明了所提出的 TLDR 的优越性；例如，TL​​DR 使用 ResNet-50 在 GTA 到城市景观上实现了 46.5 mIoU，这将之前最先进的方法提高了 1.9 mIoU。源代码可在 https://github.com/ssssshwan/TLDR 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为TLDR的框架，通过学习纹理信息来提升领域泛化分割的性能。
AI.motivation: 现有领域泛化分割方法通过强调形状信息缓解领域差异，但忽略了纹理对分割性能的关键作用，导致模型在跨领域任务中表现不足。
AI.method: 设计了两种损失函数：1) 使用ImageNet预训练模型提取纹理特征的正则化损失，防止过拟合源领域纹理；2) 通过随机风格图像进行自监督学习的纹理泛化损失，增强模型对多样化纹理的适应能力。
AI.result: 在GTA到Cityscapes数据集上，TLDR方法使用ResNet-50达到46.5 mIoU，较之前最佳方法提升1.9 mIoU。
AI.conclusion: 纹理学习在领域泛化分割中具有重要价值，TLDR框架通过双重损失机制有效提升了模型的跨领域适应能力。

authors_text: Dongyeun Lee,Jae Young Lee,Doyeon Kim,Jaehyun Choi,Jaejun Yoo,Junmo Kim
title: Fix the Noise: Disentangling Source Feature for Controllable Domain Translation
title_zh: 修复噪音：解开源特征以实现可控域转换
comment: Accepted by CVPR 2023. The code is available at https://github.com/LeeDongYeun/FixNoise. Extended from arXiv:2204.14079 (AICC workshop at CVPR 2022)
comments: Accepted by CVPR 2023. The code is available at https://github.com/LeeDongYeun/FixNoise. Extended from arXiv:2204.14079 (AICC workshop at CVPR 2022)
abstract: Recent studies show strong generative performance in domain translation especially by using transfer learning techniques on the unconditional generator. However, the control between different domain features using a single model is still challenging. Existing methods often require additional models, which is computationally demanding and leads to unsatisfactory visual quality. In addition, they have restricted control steps, which prevents a smooth transition. In this paper, we propose a new approach for high-quality domain translation with better controllability. The key idea is to preserve source features within a disentangled subspace of a target feature space. This allows our method to smoothly control the degree to which it preserves source features while generating images from an entirely new domain using only a single model. Our extensive experiments show that the proposed method can produce more consistent and realistic images than previous works and maintain precise controllability over different levels of transformation. The code is available at https://github.com/LeeDongYeun/FixNoise.
summary: Recent studies show strong generative performance in domain translation especially by using transfer learning techniques on the unconditional generator. However, the control between different domain features using a single model is still challenging. Existing methods often require additional models, which is computationally demanding and leads to unsatisfactory visual quality. In addition, they have restricted control steps, which prevents a smooth transition. In this paper, we propose a new approach for high-quality domain translation with better controllability. The key idea is to preserve source features within a disentangled subspace of a target feature space. This allows our method to smoothly control the degree to which it preserves source features while generating images from an entirely new domain using only a single model. Our extensive experiments show that the proposed method can produce more consistent and realistic images than previous works and maintain precise controllability over different levels of transformation. The code is available at https://github.com/LeeDongYeun/FixNoise.
abstract_zh: 最近的研究表明，领域翻译具有强大的生成性能，特别是通过在无条件生成器上使用迁移学习技术。然而，使用单一模型控制不同领域特征仍然具有挑战性。现有的方法通常需要额外的模型，这对计算量要求很高并且导致视觉质量不令人满意。此外，它们的控制步骤受到限制，这阻碍了平稳过渡。在本文中，我们提出了一种具有更好可控性的高质量域翻译的新方法。关键思想是将源特征保留在目标特征空间的解纠缠子空间内。这使得我们的方法能够平滑地控制保留源特征的程度，同时仅使用单个模型从全新的领域生成图像。我们的大量实验表明，所提出的方法可以产生比以前的作品更加一致和真实的图像，并在不同级别的变换上保持精确的可控性。代码可在 https://github.com/LeeDongYeun/FixNoise 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种可控域翻译方法，通过解耦源特征实现高质量图像生成。
AI.motivation: 现有方法依赖额外模型导致计算成本高且控制步骤受限，难以实现平滑转换。
AI.method: 在目标特征空间中构建解耦子空间保留源特征，通过单一模型控制特征保留程度。
AI.result: 实验表明方法生成图像更一致真实，且能精确控制不同转换层级。
AI.conclusion: 该方法通过特征解耦实现单模型可控域翻译，在质量和控制性上优于现有技术。

authors_text: Shaoxu Li,Ye Pan
title: Interactive Geometry Editing of Neural Radiance Fields
title_zh: 神经辐射场的交互式几何编辑
abstract: In this paper, we propose a method that enables interactive geometry editing for neural radiance fields manipulation. We use two proxy cages(inner cage and outer cage) to edit a scene. The inner cage defines the operation target, and the outer cage defines the adjustment space. Various operations apply to the two cages. After cage selection, operations on the inner cage lead to the desired transformation of the inner cage and adjustment of the outer cage. Users can edit the scene with translation, rotation, scaling, or combinations. The operations on the corners and edges of the cage are also supported. Our method does not need any explicit 3D geometry representations. The interactive geometry editing applies directly to the implicit neural radiance fields. Extensive experimental results demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach.
summary: In this paper, we propose a method that enables interactive geometry editing for neural radiance fields manipulation. We use two proxy cages(inner cage and outer cage) to edit a scene. The inner cage defines the operation target, and the outer cage defines the adjustment space. Various operations apply to the two cages. After cage selection, operations on the inner cage lead to the desired transformation of the inner cage and adjustment of the outer cage. Users can edit the scene with translation, rotation, scaling, or combinations. The operations on the corners and edges of the cage are also supported. Our method does not need any explicit 3D geometry representations. The interactive geometry editing applies directly to the implicit neural radiance fields. Extensive experimental results demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach.
abstract_zh: 在本文中，我们提出了一种能够对神经辐射场操作进行交互式几何编辑的方法。我们使用两个代理笼（内笼和外笼）来编辑场景。内笼限定运行目标，外笼限定调整空间。各种操作适用于两个笼子。选择保持架后，对内保持架进行操作可实现内保持架的所需变换和外保持架的调整。用户可以通过平移、旋转、缩放或组合来编辑场景。还支持对笼子角落和边缘的操作。我们的方法不需要任何显式的 3D 几何表示。交互式几何编辑直接应用于隐式神经辐射场。大量的实验结果证明了我们方法的有效性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种无需显式3D几何表示的神经辐射场交互几何编辑方法，通过内外笼子代理实现场景编辑。
AI.motivation: 现有方法难以直接对神经辐射场进行交互式几何编辑，且依赖显式几何表示限制了灵活性。
AI.method: 使用内外双笼子代理（内笼定义操作目标，外笼定义调整空间），支持平移/旋转/缩放及边角操作，直接作用于隐式神经辐射场。
AI.result: 实验验证了方法在多种场景下的有效性，实现了直观的交互式几何编辑效果。
AI.conclusion: 该方法为神经辐射场提供了无需显式几何的交互编辑方案，拓展了其应用场景。

authors_text: Tao Yang,Chuang Liu,Xiaofeng Ma,Weijia Lu,Ning Wu,Bingyang Li,Zhifei Yang,Peng Liu,Lin Sun,Xiaodong Zhang,Can Zhang
title: Indeterminate Probability Theory
title_zh: 不定概率论
abstract: Complex continuous or mixed joint distributions (e.g., P(Y | z_1, z_2, ..., z_N)) generally lack closed-form solutions, often necessitating approximations such as MCMC. This paper proposes Indeterminate Probability Theory (IPT), which makes the following contributions: (1) An observer-centered framework in which experimental outcomes are represented as distributions combining ground truth with observation error; (2) The introduction of three independence candidate axioms that enable a two-phase probabilistic inference framework; (3) The derivation of closed-form solutions for arbitrary complex joint distributions under this framework. Both the Indeterminate Probability Neural Network (IPNN) model and the non-neural multivariate time series forecasting application demonstrate IPT's effectiveness in modeling high-dimensional distributions, with successful validation up to 1000 dimensions. Importantly, IPT is consistent with classical probability theory and subsumes the frequentist equation in the limit of vanishing observation error.
summary: Complex continuous or mixed joint distributions (e.g., P(Y | z_1, z_2, ..., z_N)) generally lack closed-form solutions, often necessitating approximations such as MCMC. This paper proposes Indeterminate Probability Theory (IPT), which makes the following contributions: (1) An observer-centered framework in which experimental outcomes are represented as distributions combining ground truth with observation error; (2) The introduction of three independence candidate axioms that enable a two-phase probabilistic inference framework; (3) The derivation of closed-form solutions for arbitrary complex joint distributions under this framework. Both the Indeterminate Probability Neural Network (IPNN) model and the non-neural multivariate time series forecasting application demonstrate IPT's effectiveness in modeling high-dimensional distributions, with successful validation up to 1000 dimensions. Importantly, IPT is consistent with classical probability theory and subsumes the frequentist equation in the limit of vanishing observation error.
abstract_zh: 复杂的连续或混合联合分布（例如，P(Y | z_1, z_2, ..., z_N)）通常缺乏封闭式解，通常需要诸如 MCMC 之类的近似。本文提出了不确定概率理论（IPT），它做出了以下贡献：（1）以观察者为中心的框架，其中实验结果表示为结合真实情况与观察误差的分布； (2) 引入三个独立候选公理，实现两阶段概率推理框架； (3) 在此框架下推导任意复数联合分布的闭式解。不确定概率神经网络 (IPNN) 模型和非神经多元时间序列预测应用程序都证明了 IPT 在建模高维分布方面的有效性，并成功验证了高达 1000 个维度。重要的是，IPT 与经典概率论一致，并将频率方程纳入观测误差消失的极限。

AI.tldr: 提出不确定概率理论（IPT），以解决复杂联合分布缺乏闭式解的问题，并提供有效的高维建模方法。
AI.motivation: 传统方法如MCMC在处理高维复杂分布时效率低下，且难以融合观测误差与真实分布，亟需更高效且理论严谨的概率框架。
AI.method: 构建观察者为中心的分布融合框架，引入三独立性公理形成两阶段推理流程，推导闭式解并开发IPNN模型及非神经网络应用进行验证。
AI.result: 成功建模高达1000维的复杂分布，验证IPT与经典概率理论的兼容性，且在观测误差趋近于零时退化为频率学派方程。
AI.conclusion: IPT通过理论创新与工程实现，为高维概率建模提供兼具计算效率与理论一致性的新范式，可广泛应用于多领域复杂系统分析。

authors_text: Muhammad Kazim,Michael Muldoon,Kwang-Ki K. Kim
title: Adaptive Goal Management System of Robots
title_zh: 机器人自适应目标管理系统
abstract: This paper considers the problem of managing single or multiple robots and proposes a cloud-based robot fleet manager, Adaptive Goal Management (AGM) System, for teams of unmanned mobile robots. The AGM system uses an adaptive goal execution approach and provides a restful API for communication between single or multiple robots, enabling real-time monitoring and control. The overarching goal of AGM is to coordinate single or multiple robots to productively complete tasks in an environment. There are some existing works that provide various solutions for managing single or multiple robots, but the proposed AGM system is designed to be adaptable and scalable, making it suitable for managing multiple heterogeneous robots in diverse environments with dynamic changes. The proposed AGM system presents a versatile and efficient solution for managing single or multiple robots across multiple industries, such as healthcare, agriculture, airports, manufacturing, and logistics. By enhancing the capabilities of these robots and enabling seamless task execution, the AGM system offers a powerful tool for facilitating complex operations. The effectiveness of the proposed AGM system is demonstrated through simulation experiments in diverse environments using ROS1 with Gazebo. The results show that the AGM system efficiently manages the allocated tasks and missions. Tests conducted in the manufacturing industry have shown promising results in task and mission management for both a single Mobile Industrial Robot and multiple Turtlebot3 robots. To provide further insights, a supplementary video showcasing the experiments can be found at https://github.com/mukmalone/ AdaptiveGoalManagement.

abstract_zh: 本文考虑了管理单个或多个机器人的问题，并提出了一种适用于无人移动机器人团队的基于云的机器人车队管理器——自适应目标管理（AGM）系统。 AGM系统采用自适应目标执行方法，并提供Restful API用于单个或多个机器人之间的通信，从而实现实时监控和控制。 AGM 的总体目标是协调单个或多个机器人在环境中高效地完成任务。现有的一些工作为管理单个或多个机器人提供了各种解决方案，但所提出的 AGM 系统被设计为具有适应性和可扩展性，使其适合在动态变化的不同环境中管理多个异构机器人。拟议的 AGM 系统提供了一种多功能且高效的解决方案，用于管理多个行业（例如医疗保健、农业、机场、制造和物流）的单个或多个机器人。通过增强这些机器人的功能并实现无缝任务执行，AGM 系统为促进复杂操作提供了强大的工具。通过使用 ROS1 和 Gazebo 在不同环境中进行模拟实验，证明了所提出的 AGM 系统的有效性。结果表明，年度股东大会系统有效地管理了分配的任务和使命。在制造业中进行的测试显示，单个移动工业机器人和多个 Turtlebot3 机器人在任务和任务管理方面取得了可喜的成果。为了提供进一步的见解，可以在 https://github.com/mukmalone/AdaptiveGoalManagement 上找到展示实验的补充视频。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于云的机器人集群管理框架——自适应目标管理系统（AGM），用于协调单个或多个无人移动机器人高效完成任务。
AI.motivation: 现有机器人管理系统难以适应异构机器人和动态环境，缺乏可扩展性与灵活性，需设计更通用的解决方案以应对多行业复杂任务需求。
AI.method: 采用自适应目标执行机制与RESTful API实现机器人间通信，结合ROS1与Gazebo进行多场景仿真测试，并在制造业中验证单机与多机协作性能。
AI.result: 仿真与实验证明AGM系统能有效管理任务分配，单机Mobile Industrial Robot及多台Turtlebot3机器人测试均取得良好效果。
AI.conclusion: AGM系统为医疗、农业、制造等多行业提供高效机器人管理方案，其适应性与扩展性通过仿真与实际测试得到验证。
